stories.title: Commonwealth Games 2026 opens in Glasgow tonight — Mirabai Chanu and Lovlina Borgohain lead India's charge
stories.what_happened: The 2026 Commonwealth Games officially open tonight at Glasgow's OVO Hydro arena, with India's contingent led into the stadium by two Olympic medallists: weightlifter Mirabai Chanu carrying the flag and boxer Lovlina Borgohain carrying the baton. Ahead of the ceremony, India's lawn bowls team was already competing today, with men's singles player Putul Sonowal — ranked 21st in the world — facing Canada's Ryan Bester, and the women's pairs team taking on Malta.
key_points: Opening ceremony is tonight at Glasgow's OVO Hydro, 10:30pm IST
Olympic medallists Mirabai Chanu (flag bearer) and Lovlina Borgohain (baton bearer) lead India
India's bowls team is already in action today ahead of the ceremony
This is Glasgow's second time hosting the Games, after 2014

depth.background: The Commonwealth Games is a multi-sport event held every four years among athletes from Commonwealth nations — mostly countries with historical ties to the British Empire, including India, the UK, Australia, and dozens of others. Glasgow previously hosted the Games in 2014, and India has traditionally used the event as a proving ground and confidence-builder ahead of the Olympics, especially in weightlifting, boxing, wrestling and shooting.

stories.title: IMD red-flags heavy rain and 70 km/h winds across 12 states, with Himachal Pradesh and J&K under red alert
stories.what_happened: The IMD issued an alert for heavy rain and winds up to 70 km/h across 12 states including Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh through the day. Himachal Pradesh and Jammu & Kashmir are under a red alert, while parts of western Madhya Pradesh (Indore, Ujjain, Ratlam) could see over 115mm of rain in isolated spots — enough to trigger flash floods in narrow streets. Rain is also continuing over Delhi, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
key_points: IMD alert: heavy rain, 70 km/h winds across 12 states
Red alert in Himachal Pradesh and Jammu & Kashmir
Up to 115mm rain possible in west Madhya Pradesh
Flash-flood and landslide risk in hill states

depth.background: India's monsoon, roughly June to September, brings the bulk of the country's annual rainfall in a few intense months. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issues colour-coded alerts — green, yellow, orange, red — as storm systems move across regions, with red being the most severe, meaning 'take action', not just 'be aware'.

stories.title: Alonso and Newey headline today's FIA media day in Hungary — a sign of how much is riding on Aston Martin's upgrade
stories.what_happened: Fernando Alonso will lead Thursday's drivers' press conference alongside Alpine's Franco Colapinto and Williams' Carlos Sainz, while Aston Martin team principal Adrian Newey — the sport's most famous car designer — headlines the team representatives' session. The timing lines up with Aston Martin's season-defining upgrade debuting at the Hungaroring this weekend, after a season that has tested both Alonso's patience and Newey's reputation following his own health scare earlier this year.
key_points: Fernando Alonso and Aston Martin boss Adrian Newey lead Thursday's FIA press conferences
The pairing is notable because Aston Martin's biggest upgrade of the season debuts this weekend
The FIA says driver picks are random, but timing suggests otherwise
Max Verstappen, by contrast, isn't part of today's media duties

depth.background: Each F1 race weekend, the FIA holds press conferences where a handful of drivers and team bosses are selected to answer media questions — officially at random, though the choices often line up with whichever storylines are biggest that weekend. Aston Martin has struggled through a difficult season and has pinned its hopes on a major upgrade package — new chassis, nose and aerodynamics — that lands at this week's Hungarian Grand Prix.

stories.title: Alonso and Newey summoned to face the media at Hungary — right as Aston Martin bets its season on a revised chassis
stories.what_happened: Alonso and Newey have both been picked for the FIA's official pre-race media sessions at this weekend's Hungarian Grand Prix — Alonso alongside Alpine's Franco Colapinto and Williams' Carlos Sainz in Thursday's drivers' conference, and Newey alongside technical staff from Mercedes and Red Bull in Friday's team-representative session. The FIA maintains its selection process is random, but it comes as Aston Martin is reported to be bringing a substantially revised car chassis to Hungary, described as the first of a two-phase upgrade plan aimed at rescuing their season.
key_points: Alonso faces media today, Newey tomorrow, at Hungary GP
FIA calls selection random, but timing fuels speculation
Aston Martin reportedly brings a revised chassis this weekend
Seen as phase one of a two-part plan to save their season

depth.background: Fernando Alonso is a two-time world champion now driving for Aston Martin, a team that recruited legendary designer Adrian Newey specifically to turn its underperforming car into a race winner. Aston Martin has struggled for competitive pace this season, and Alonso has been publicly candid that his own F1 future depends on whether the team — and the sport's incoming 2027 engine rules — give him a real shot at winning again before he considers retirement.

thread.previously: We told you Fernando Alonso said whether he stays in F1 beyond his current deal depends more on the sport's 2027 engine rule changes than on Aston Martin's form specifically.
thread.changed: Alonso and Aston Martin's technical boss Adrian Newey have now both been selected for the FIA's official Hungarian Grand Prix media sessions — Alonso in Thursday's drivers' press conference, Newey in Friday's team-representative one. While the FIA says selection is random, the timing lines up with speculation that Aston Martin is bringing a significantly revised chassis to Hungary as the first phase of a two-part plan to save their season, which is directly relevant to whether Alonso sees enough progress to stay.

prev_ref.title: Alonso says his F1 future rests on the rules, not on Aston Martin — and 2027's engine shake-up is the real deciding factor

stories.title: Shreyas Iyer chases his first win as India T20I captain against Zimbabwe, after seven straight defeats in charge
stories.what_happened: India begins a three-match T20I series against Zimbabwe at the Harare Sports Club today, with Shreyas Iyer captaining a young, largely inexperienced squad that includes four uncapped players. Iyer has lost all seven matches he's captained so far and is seeking his first win in the role. Zimbabwe, led by Sikandar Raza, will look to capitalise on India fielding a second-string side.
key_points: 1st T20I today at Harare Sports Club, 4:30pm IST
Iyer is winless in seven matches as India's T20I captain
India lost series in both Ireland and England recently
Squad features four uncapped, largely young players

depth.background: Shreyas Iyer was handed India's T20I captaincy as part of a broader generational transition after senior players stepped back from the format. Since taking over, the results haven't gone his way — India has lost series in Ireland (2-0) and then in England (4-0), leaving Iyer under real pressure heading into this tour, even though the current squad is deliberately youth-heavy and experimental.

stories.title: India's state fuel giant HPCL posted a ₹11,526 crore loss — even as its revenue jumped 21%
stories.what_happened: HPCL reported a standalone net loss of ₹11,526.41 crore for the quarter ended June 2026, its consolidated loss coming in at ₹12,264.67 crore — a sharp reversal from a ₹4,901.50 crore profit the previous quarter. Despite this, revenue actually grew 21% to ₹1,45,126 crore, and its refineries performed strongly, running at 107% of rated capacity. The loss came entirely from the marketing side: HPCL kept selling petrol, diesel and LPG to consumers at prices that didn't cover its now-higher input costs.
key_points: HPCL's Q1 loss: ₹11,526 crore standalone (₹12,265 crore consolidated)
Revenue still grew 21% year-on-year to ₹1.45 lakh crore
Cause: it sold petrol, diesel and LPG below cost while crude oil got more expensive
The Iran war's impact on oil prices is the direct trigger
Its refineries ran at 107% of capacity — the refining side alone did fine

depth.background: HPCL (Hindustan Petroleum Corporation) is one of India's three big state-run oil-marketing companies — they refine crude oil into fuel and sell it at the pump. India keeps retail fuel prices relatively stable for consumers even when global crude oil prices spike, which means these companies sometimes have to sell fuel for less than it costs them to make and deliver it. The government generally expects them to absorb short-term losses rather than pass a price shock straight to drivers.

stories.title: Parliament collapses again within 2 minutes as opposition ties Wangchuk's hunger strike to a hospitalized student protester and demands a minister's resignation
stories.what_happened: Both houses of Parliament convened at 11am today and were adjourned again within two minutes amid uproar, extending the disruption to a fourth day. Reports today add two new, serious details to the story: a young woman injured during the July 20 police crackdown on the 'Chalo Sansad' march is now on a ventilator at RML Hospital in Delhi, and the Delhi High Court has approved shifting Wangchuk — now 25 days into his fast — to Medanta Hospital for round-the-clock care. The opposition is using both developments to demand the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.
key_points: Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha both adjourned within 2 minutes of convening at 11am today — Day 4 of chaos
Opposition now demands Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resign over the 'Chalo Sansad' march crackdown
A young protester injured in that police action is reported on a ventilator at RML Hospital
Delhi HC has separately allowed moving hunger-striker Sonam Wangchuk to Medanta Hospital for continuous care

depth.background: India's Parliament has been in near-continuous disruption this week (see our earlier coverage of Day 3) over the NEET exam-paper-leak scandal and the government's handling of student protests demanding accountability. On July 20, a student-led 'Chalo Sansad' ('March to Parliament') protest was met with a police crackdown; opposition leaders including Rahul Gandhi were briefly detained the next day while protesting outside PM Modi's residence over it. Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk has been on a hunger strike over the same NEET issue and was hospitalized mid-strike.

thread.previously: We reported the third straight day of chaos, with Lok Sabha adjourned again as the government said it was ready to discuss the NEET leak and blamed Congress for the disruption.
thread.changed: Two serious new facts have emerged: a protester injured in the July 20 crackdown is now on a ventilator, and Wangchuk — now 25 days into his fast — has been cleared by the Delhi High Court to move to Medanta Hospital. The opposition has escalated to demanding the Education Minister's resignation, and today's session was adjourned within 2 minutes.

prev_ref.title: Parliament's third day of chaos: Lok Sabha adjourned again as government says it's ready to discuss NEET leak, blames Congress for conditions

stories.title: The Commonwealth Games open in Glasgow today — India sends a 126-athlete squad led by Neeraj Chopra, with Mirabai Chanu carrying the flag
stories.what_happened: The 2026 Commonwealth Games officially open today in Glasgow with a ceremony at the OVO Hydro arena, running through August 2. India is sending a 126-athlete contingent, headlined by two-time Olympic medallist javelin thrower Neeraj Chopra, Tokyo Olympics silver medallist weightlifter Mirabai Chanu (who is India's flag bearer), and boxer Lovlina Borgohain (baton bearer). The ceremony features Scottish musical acts including Tom Walker and KT Tunstall alongside the traditional athletes' parade.
key_points: Opening ceremony tonight at Glasgow's OVO Hydro
126 Indian athletes competing, July 23 to August 2
Neeraj Chopra (javelin) is India's biggest medal hope
Mirabai Chanu is flag bearer, Lovlina Borgohain the baton bearer

depth.background: The Commonwealth Games is a multi-sport event held every four years among countries that were once part of the British Empire — think of it as a smaller, regional version of the Olympics, with around 70 nations and territories taking part. India has historically done well here, especially in weightlifting, wrestling, badminton and boxing, and uses it as a mid-cycle benchmark between Olympic Games. This edition is being hosted in Glasgow, Scotland.

stories.title: Pakistan's women start a 3-match ODI series in Sri Lanka today — against a hot Sri Lankan side
stories.what_happened: Sri Lanka Women host Pakistan Women in the first of three ODIs at the Mahinda Rajapaksa International Cricket Stadium in Hambantota, starting today. All three matches (July 23, 25, 28) are being played at the same venue. Sri Lanka, led by Chamari Athapaththu, come into the series having just won an away ODI series against Bangladesh 2-1.
key_points: Sri Lanka Women vs Pakistan Women, 1st ODI, in Hambantota today
3-match series runs July 23, 25 and 28, all at the same venue
Sri Lanka arrive fresh off a 2-1 ODI series win away in Bangladesh
Sri Lanka captained by veteran Chamari Athapaththu

depth.background: Women's cricket has been getting a steadily bigger international calendar in recent years, with more bilateral series between full-member cricket nations. Sri Lanka and Pakistan are both mid-tier teams in the women's game, building toward future ICC events, and series like this matter for team rankings and player development even when they don't get the attention men's cricket does.

stories.title: Assam's floods just got deadlier — 10 more died overnight, taking the toll to 41
stories.what_happened: Assam's disaster management authorities confirmed 10 more deaths in the 24 hours to Thursday, including four children, pushing the season's death toll to 41. The worst-hit district, Sivasagar, alone accounts for over 3.9 lakh of the 6.53 lakh people affected across 11 districts. Rising water levels in the Brahmaputra and its tributary the Disang continue to threaten new areas even as rescue operations continue.
key_points: 41 dead so far this flood season, including 4 children in the last 24 hours alone
6.53 lakh (653,000) people affected across 11 districts
Sivasagar district is worst-hit; Army, NDRF and SDRF have rescued 8,400+ people
Over 3.1 lakh people are sheltering in 487 relief camps

depth.background: Assam, in India's northeast, sits on the floodplain of the Brahmaputra river and floods almost every monsoon (June–September) as rain swells the river and its tributaries. Some years are routine; some are severe. This is a separate flood event from the one that hit Kashmir and Nagaland earlier this week — different rivers, different region, same monsoon system driving both.

stories.title: Google just raised its AI spending plan to $205 billion for 2026 — and its stock dropped anyway
stories.what_happened: On its Wednesday earnings call, Alphabet said it now expects to spend $195–205 billion on AI infrastructure in 2026, above the roughly $186 billion Wall Street analysts had penciled in. The increase came alongside decent Q2 results: revenue of $119.8 billion (above expectations) and a cloud-computing beat, though earnings per share of $2.85 missed slightly. CFO Anat Ashkenazi said the company is 'accelerating' its build-out because AI demand keeps outrunning supply.
key_points: Alphabet (Google's parent) now says it'll spend up to $205B in 2026, up from ~$190B
Cloud revenue beat expectations; core Search fell just short
Shares fell nearly 5% after hours despite the beat — investors fear over-spending
It's the third time this year Alphabet has raised this number

depth.background: Every major AI company — Google, Microsoft, Meta, Amazon — is in a race to build enough data centers and chips to run AI models at scale. That building is called 'capital expenditure' (capex): money spent on physical stuff (chips, buildings, power) rather than day-to-day costs. Wall Street has been watching nervously all year, worried these companies are spending faster than AI is actually making them money.

stories.title: McLaren pulls Piastri from Hungary FP1 — reigning F2 champion Fornaroli gets the seat under F1's rookie rule
stories.what_happened: Oscar Piastri will not drive in Friday's first practice session at the Hungarian Grand Prix. McLaren junior driver Leonardo Fornaroli, the reigning Formula 2 champion, takes his seat instead to satisfy F1's mandatory rookie rule. It's McLaren's turn in a wave of similar swaps this weekend, with Alpine, Haas, and Cadillac also fielding rookies or reserves in practice.
key_points: McLaren's Oscar Piastri sits out first practice at the Hungarian Grand Prix
Leonardo Fornaroli, McLaren's junior and reigning F2 champion, drives Piastri's car instead
It's mandated by F1's rookie rule, not a performance issue
Comes as McLaren brings a major upgrade package to Hungary to chase Mercedes and Ferrari

depth.background: F1 rules require every team to run a driver with two or fewer prior race starts in at least two Friday practice sessions per season, giving young talent track time in a real car. Fornaroli already drove Lando Norris's car at a practice session in Barcelona last month; this is his second outing.

stories.title: Houthis strike two Saudi oil tankers, leaving one ablaze in the Red Sea
stories.what_happened: The Houthis said they hit two Saudi Arabian oil tankers, the Encelia and the Layla, with a mix of ballistic and cruise missiles and drones as they sailed the Red Sea about 70 nautical miles southwest of Saudi Arabia's Al Shuqaiq. Saudi Arabia's state news agency confirmed a fire broke out on the Encelia's bow after it was struck, but said all crew members were safe and the vessel was not in danger of sinking.
key_points: Houthis fired missiles and drones at Saudi-flagged tankers Encelia and Layla
Saudi state media confirms a fire on Encelia's bow; all crew safe
Houthis say the ships broke a blockade they declared against Saudi Arabia on Monday
First real attacks under that new blockade — a fresh front beyond their old campaign

depth.background: Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthi movement has attacked shipping in the Red Sea since 2023, originally targeting vessels it linked to Israel or its allies over the Gaza war. This week that campaign took a new turn: on Monday the Houthis declared a naval blockade specifically against Saudi Arabia, in retaliation for a Saudi blockade on Yemen and a strike on Sanaa's international airport. Today's tanker attacks are the first strikes carried out under that new, Saudi-specific blockade — separate from, and in addition to, the Houthis' long-running campaign against Israel-linked ships.

thread.previously: Yesterday the Houthis announced they were imposing a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia in retaliation for a Saudi blockade on Yemen and an attack on Sanaa's airport — at that point it was a declaration, with no attacks yet reported.
thread.changed: Today the Houthis acted on that declaration for the first time, striking two named Saudi oil tankers and setting one on fire — moving the blockade from threat to reality.

prev_ref.title: Yemen's Houthis open a second shipping front, blockading the Red Sea's Bab al-Mandeb Strait

stories.title: India's biggest MIM-parts maker Indo-MIM opens its ₹3,811 crore IPO today
stories.what_happened: Indo-MIM's ₹3,811.21 crore initial public offering (IPO) opened for public subscription today and runs through Monday, July 27. The price band is set at ₹461-485 per share, with a minimum retail investment of about ₹14,550. Of the total issue, only ₹500 crore is a fresh issue (new money raised for the company, earmarked largely to repay debt); the remaining ₹3,311.21 crore is an Offer for Sale, meaning existing shareholders are selling their own stock and pocketing the proceeds. Shares are expected to list on the BSE and NSE around July 30. The grey market premium — an unofficial, pre-listing gauge of investor demand — is running around 37-39% above the upper price band.
key_points: Bengaluru-based Indo-MIM's IPO opens today, closes Monday July 27
Price band set at ₹461-485 per share; listing expected around July 30
Grey market premium running near 37-39%, signalling strong early demand
Only ₹500 crore of the ₹3,811 crore issue is fresh money for the company

depth.background: Indo-MIM is a Bengaluru-based precision engineering company and the world's largest player in Metal Injection Molding (MIM) — a manufacturing process that packs metal powder into a mould to mass-produce small, complex parts (tiny gears, brackets, connectors) more cheaply than traditional machining. It supplies components to global automakers, medical-device makers and other industrial clients, and has built its pitch around India's growing role as a precision-manufacturing export hub.

stories.title: India-US trade deal talks reach the 'last 1%' of the legal text, with a tariff truce set to expire tomorrow
stories.what_happened: As of today, officials describe the agreement's text as roughly 99% complete — the broad framework covering market access, digital trade, supply-chain cooperation and reduced non-tariff barriers is largely settled — but the single biggest sticking point, the actual tariff rate India will face, remains unresolved. The current temporary 10% tariff arrangement expires tomorrow, July 24, though Trump has set a separate, later deadline of August 1 for several countries including India to finalize deals before facing steeper tariffs.
key_points: Negotiators say the deal's overall framework is largely ready but the final tariff numbers are still unresolved
A temporary 10% US tariff arrangement on Indian goods expires July 24 — tomorrow
Trump has separately set August 1 as a broader deadline for countries to sign or face steep tariffs
India wants a better tariff rate than the one given to Southeast Asian competitors

depth.background: India and the US have spent months negotiating a Bilateral Trade Agreement aimed at cutting tariffs and expanding market access in both directions. Washington had placed a temporary 10% tariff on Indian goods as a placeholder while talks continued, due to lapse this week — after which, without a deal, tariffs could jump sharply, echoing the tariff plan Trump has already floated for other sectors like pharmaceuticals (covered in our earlier reporting).

thread.previously: We reported India and the US racing to close a trade deal before Thursday's temporary tariff deadline, with India still holding out for better terms.
thread.changed: Officials now describe the deal text as roughly 99% complete, with only the final tariff rate unresolved, as the temporary tariff arrangement's expiry (tomorrow, July 24) approaches.

prev_ref.title: India and the US race to close a trade deal before Thursday's tariff deadline — India still holding out

stories.title: India carry cricket's longest active T20I winless streak into today's series opener in Zimbabwe
stories.what_happened: India play the first of three T20Is against Zimbabwe today at Harare Sports Club. Coming in, India — the reigning world champions — are in the middle of their longest-ever winless streak in T20I history, and Iyer is winless in seven matches since becoming captain. Historically, India lead the head-to-head 11-3, but all three of Zimbabwe's wins have come at this exact ground, making today's venue a genuine banana skin.
key_points: India play Zimbabwe's 1st T20I today in Harare at 4:30pm IST
India, the reigning T20 World Cup champions, are on their longest-ever winless run in the format
Captain Shreyas Iyer has yet to win a single match since taking over — 7 straight defeats
Zimbabwe have historically only beaten India at this exact venue, Harare Sports Club

depth.background: India won the T20 World Cup earlier this year but have since gone into an unprecedented slump in the format under new white-ball captain Shreyas Iyer, who took over as part of a leadership transition. Ahead of this Zimbabwe tour, injuries to spinner Varun Chakravarthy and pacer Harshit Rana forced India to call up uncapped players Ravi Bishnoi and Prince Yadav, adding inexperience to an already out-of-sorts squad (as we reported earlier this week).

thread.previously: We reported India calling up uncapped players Ravi Bishnoi and Prince Yadav after injuries to Varun Chakravarthy and Harshit Rana, as India arrived in Zimbabwe on cricket's longest active T20I winless streak.
thread.changed: The series opener is now underway today, the first real test of whether the reshuffled, injury-hit squad can end the losing run.

prev_ref.title: India call up Ravi Bishnoi and Prince Yadav after Varun Chakravarthy and Harshit Rana are ruled out injured

stories.title: Nepal's World Cup qualification hopes are on the line as they face the Netherlands in a must-win tri-series match
stories.what_happened: Netherlands and Nepal play each other today, July 23, in Utrecht as part of a tri-series with Namibia running from July 21 to 31, 2026, part of the ongoing League 2 competition. Nepal currently sit fifth in the League 2 standings with 28 points from 28 matches played — just outside the top four required for direct qualification to the 2027 World Cup Qualifier.
key_points: Netherlands host Nepal today in Utrecht in the ICC Men's Cricket World Cup League 2
Nepal sit 5th in the League 2 table with 28 points from 28 matches — outside the direct-qualifying top 4
Namibia, Nepal and Netherlands are playing a tri-series through July 31 for crucial ranking points
Top 4 finishers in League 2 qualify directly for the 2027 World Cup Qualifier

depth.background: The ICC Cricket World Cup League 2 is a multi-year one-day-international league where lower-ranked cricket nations (like Nepal, Namibia, Oman, and the USA) play each other over dozens of matches, with the top finishers earning a route toward qualifying for the 50-over Cricket World Cup — a huge deal for teams outside cricket's traditional 'Full Member' elite, who otherwise rarely get ODI status matches at all. Nepal has a passionate, fast-growing cricket following and has been pushing for years to break into the sport's top tier.

stories.title: Wildfires kill three firefighters and force thousands to flee across Sicily, France and Spain
stories.what_happened: Wildfires swept across southern Europe this week, killing three firefighters and forcing thousands of evacuations. In Sicily, where temperatures topped 40°C, about 6,000 firefighters and civil-protection workers are battling roughly 50 fires with the help of water-bombing aircraft; one firefighter died after falling ill on the job. In southwestern France, two firefighters were killed tackling a blaze that has burned over 1,000 hectares (about 2,500 acres) near the Arcachon Basin close to Bordeaux. In Spain's Guadalajara province, a forest fire has raged out of control for six days, and authorities have evacuated more than 1,200 people, with new emergency evacuation orders issued near Toledo, south of Madrid.
key_points: Two firefighters died battling a blaze near Bordeaux, France; one died in Sicily
Around 6,000 personnel are fighting roughly 50 fires across Sicily alone
A Spanish blaze near Toledo has burned for six days, forcing 1,200+ evacuations
Temperatures above 40°C and dry winds are driving the flames

depth.background: Southern Europe has faced repeated summer heatwaves and wildfire outbreaks in recent years, as hotter, drier conditions extend the fire season and make blazes harder to control. Sicily, southwestern France, and central Spain are all currently under extreme heat, with strong winds fanning flames across parched land.

stories.title: Government offers Sonam Wangchuk compensation and a Parliament debate — but he's still holding out for a written promise
stories.what_happened: Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh, who visited Wangchuk in the ICU, have now responded to his letter. They told him the government would 'positively consider' compensating the families of students who died by suicide after the NEET leak, hold a genuine discussion in Parliament on exam accountability, and consider Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. But those are verbal assurances, not the written, binding guarantee Wangchuk specifically asked for — that no student or young protester will face legal or punitive action. He has made clear he won't end his fast without that written commitment. Doctors say he has lost about 21 pounds and warn of a real risk of organ damage the longer the strike continues.
key_points: Ministers Nadda and Singh offered compensation for students who died by suicide over the NEET leak
They also floated a real Parliament debate and 'considering' Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation
Wangchuk says none of that is enough — he wants it in writing that protesting students won't face legal action
His hunger strike is now in its 25th day, with doctors warning of possible organ damage

depth.background: Sonam Wangchuk, a Ladakh-based education reformer and climate activist, has been on hunger strike since June 28 in solidarity with the Gen Z-led protest movement demanding accountability for leaks in the NEET medical-entrance exam, including Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. He is being treated in the ICU at Delhi's Medanta Hospital. Yesterday, after union ministers visited him in hospital, he wrote to them setting a specific condition for ending his fast: a written guarantee that no legal action would be taken against student protesters.

thread.previously: Wangchuk wrote to the Centre saying he would only end his 24-day hunger strike if given a written assurance that no legal action would be taken against student protesters, a day after ministers visited him in the ICU.
thread.changed: Ministers have now responded with concrete verbal offers — compensation for grieving families, a real Parliament debate, and considering Pradhan's resignation — but Wangchuk says he still needs it in writing before he'll end his fast, now in its 25th day.

prev_ref.title: Sonam Wangchuk sets a new condition to end his hunger strike — from his ICU bed

stories.title: The Global Super League kicks off in Guyana today — a $1 million showdown between the world's franchise-league champions
stories.what_happened: The 2026 Global Super League begins today at the Guyana National Stadium in Providence, with Lahore Qalandars facing Perth Scorchers XI in the opening match. Five teams will compete over ten days for a $1 million prize pool, with San Francisco Unicorns making their tournament debut as Major League Cricket's representative — a sign of US franchise cricket's growing standing on the world stage.
key_points: Five teams from five different T20 leagues compete for a $1 million prize pool
Lahore Qalandars (PSL) face Perth Scorchers XI (BBL) in today's opener
San Francisco Unicorns represent US franchise cricket alongside Major League Cricket's rise
Tournament runs July 23 to August 1 at Guyana's Providence stadium

depth.background: The Global Super League, organized by Cricket West Indies, pits the champion or representative teams of major T20 franchise leagues against each other: the Caribbean Premier League (CPL), Pakistan Super League (PSL), Big Bash League (BBL), International League T20 (ILT20), and now Major League Cricket (MLC) from the US. It's now in its third edition, and this year's field includes defending champions Guyana Amazon Warriors, Desert Vipers, Lahore Qalandars, Perth Scorchers XI, and San Francisco Unicorns.

stories.title: India's under-19s hold on for a draw as Sri Lanka survive a tense final day in Colombo
stories.what_happened: After India declared their second innings on 208 for 3, Sri Lanka's under-19s were left needing to survive the rest of the match without losing all their wickets. They did exactly that, batting out the final day to end the match in a draw. The result means the two-match series finishes level, since the first Youth Test between the sides also ended in a draw, with India's Lakshay Raichandani scoring a double century in that game.
key_points: The 2nd Youth Test ended in a draw at the R Premadasa Stadium
India posted a huge 411 in the first innings, then declared their second innings
Sri Lanka's under-19s batted out the final day to save the match
The two-match series is now level after a draw in the first Test too

depth.background: India's under-19 team has been touring Sri Lanka for a two-match Youth Test series — the junior version of a Test match, used to develop future international players. India piled up a commanding first-innings total of 411 thanks to a 122-run partnership between Kushagra Ojha and captain Yashbardhan Chauhan, putting Sri Lanka's under-19s under serious pressure to save the game.

thread.previously: India posted a first-innings 411, and Sri Lanka's under-19s were fighting to survive the rest of the match to avoid defeat.
thread.changed: Sri Lanka's under-19s successfully batted out the final day, and the match ended in a draw, meaning the two-match series finishes level at 0-0.

prev_ref.title: Sri Lanka's under-19s fight to save the Youth Test in Colombo after India's first-innings 411

stories.title: F1 drivers meet the FIA today over stewards' inconsistent penalty calls after Spa's Hamilton-Leclerc controversy
stories.what_happened: At the Belgian Grand Prix, Lewis Hamilton was given a five-second time penalty after clipping the rear of George Russell's car on the opening lap. A few laps later, in what drivers say was a very similar situation, Charles Leclerc was cleared with no action after contact with Oscar Piastri at the same corner. The differing outcomes have frustrated the grid, and F1 drivers are using an already-scheduled meeting with FIA officials at this weekend's Hungarian Grand Prix to press for clearer, more consistently applied rules.
key_points: Hamilton got a 5-second penalty for a first-lap clash with Russell at the Belgian GP
Leclerc got no penalty for a very similar incident with Piastri a few laps later
Drivers say stewards misapplied the rules and want the guidelines clarified
The meeting was already scheduled for this weekend, but Spa put it under a spotlight

depth.background: F1 stewards judge on-track incidents using a set of 'Driving Standards Guidelines' meant to make penalty decisions consistent from race to race. In practice, drivers have long complained that similar incidents get different rulings depending on the stewards on duty that weekend, which makes it hard to know where the racing line is before a penalty gets handed out.

stories.title: The Sikkim tunnel disaster death toll triples to 22 as overnight rescue teams pull nine more bodies from the mud
stories.what_happened: On July 20, a sudden burst of suspected methane gas trapped inside the rock triggered an explosion inside the under-construction Head Race Tunnel at Samardung, filling it with toxic fumes and collapsing part of the structure. As of today, the confirmed death toll has climbed to 22 after rescue crews recovered nine more bodies overnight. The National Disaster Response Force (NDRF), Sikkim's State Disaster Response Force (SDRF), and a specialist mine-rescue team from Eastern Coalfields Limited are working through hazardous, mud-clogged conditions to reach any workers still trapped.
key_points: Death toll rises from 7 to 22 after overnight recovery
Accident hit the NHPC Teesta Stage VI tunnel on July 20
Mud and water are hampering the rescue of remaining workers
Sikkim CM promises compensation and a formal inquiry

depth.background: The tunnel is part of the NHPC Teesta Stage VI hydropower project, one of several dam and tunnel projects being built in Sikkim's mountainous, geologically unstable terrain to generate electricity from the region's rivers. Building tunnels through the Himalayas is notoriously risky — the rock can trap pockets of gas, and India has seen fatal tunnel collapses before, including a prolonged rescue in neighbouring Uttarakhand in past years.

thread.previously: We told you a suspected methane gas explosion had collapsed part of an under-construction hydropower tunnel in Sikkim's Namchi district, killing at least 7 workers with many more feared trapped inside.
thread.changed: The confirmed death toll has now nearly tripled to 22, after rescue teams recovered nine more bodies overnight. Rescue operations, involving India's NDRF and SDRF disaster-response forces plus a specialist team from Eastern Coalfields Limited, have been repeatedly hampered by mud and water accumulation inside the tunnel. Sikkim's Chief Minister has announced compensation for victims' families and promised a formal inquiry.

prev_ref.title: A methane blast collapses a hydro-project tunnel in Sikkim — at least 7 killed, many feared trapped

stories.title: Phil Salt stars as Welsh Fire beat Southern Brave in a tense men's Hundred finish
stories.what_happened: In the men's Hundred fixture at Southampton, Southern Brave posted 135 for 7. Welsh Fire chased it down for the loss of only 4 wickets, getting home with just 2 balls to spare in their 100-ball innings. Phil Salt was named Player of the Match for his innings at the top of Welsh Fire's order.
key_points: Welsh Fire chased down Southern Brave's 135/7, winning by 6 wickets with 2 balls to spare
Phil Salt named Player of the Match for his knock at the top of the order
Southern Brave's women's team won their own match earlier the same day
Both teams' games are part of The Hundred, England's short-format summer competition

depth.background: The Hundred is an English cricket competition using a shortened '100-ball' format (instead of the usual 120-ball T20), designed to be quicker and more accessible for new fans. Each men's and women's team plays a full round of fixtures over the English summer, and results in one gender's match are entirely separate from the other's, even on the same match day.

stories.title: Poll: Ukrainians wanted the general gone, not the tech minister — Fedorov's trust rating surges past Zelensky's amid daily protests
stories.what_happened: New polling from Rating Group, fielded July 20-21, shows Ukrainians' trust in Fedorov jumped from 35% to 65% in a single week — now higher than trust in Zelensky himself — while trust in Syrskyi fell from 39% to 23% over the same period. Separately, 72% of respondents said they did not support Fedorov's dismissal, versus just 5% who agreed with it. Thousands have rallied in Kyiv and other cities daily since the firing, demanding his reappointment.
key_points: Poll: Fedorov's trust rating jumped from 35% to 65% in a week
72% of Ukrainians opposed his dismissal, just 5% backed it
Thousands protest nationwide daily, demanding his reappointment
Army chief Syrskyi's trust fell from 39% to 23% over same period

depth.background: Mykhailo Fedorov built Ukraine's wartime drone and tech program from scratch and became one of the most visible, popular faces of the war effort. Yesterday's coverage described Zelensky firing both Fedorov and army chief Syrskyi within the same week and installing a younger war cabinet, reportedly to resolve a personal clash between the two men.

thread.previously: Zelensky fired both defense minister Fedorov and army chief Syrskyi within the same week and installed a younger war cabinet, reportedly over a personal rift between the two men.
thread.changed: New polling shows the public wanted the opposite outcome — Fedorov's trust rating surged to 65% (above Zelensky's own), 72% opposed his dismissal, and thousands are protesting nationwide demanding he be reinstated.

prev_ref.title: Zelenskyy fires Ukraine's top general and defense minister in one week, installs a younger war cabinet

stories.title: Trump threatens to bomb an Iranian bridge or power plant every time Iran hits a ship in the Strait of Hormuz
stories.what_happened: President Trump posted a public warning that from now on, any time Iran shoots at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz — with a missile, rocket, drone or anything else — the US will respond by destroying one bridge or power plant inside Iran, including ones near the capital, Tehran. A US strike has already hit the small Iranian island of Larak, which sits in the Strait. Iran's health ministry says US strikes have killed more than 50 people and injured nearly 600 over the past month. A senior Iranian official responded that Tehran would strike 'infrastructure and bridges across the region' if the US follows through.
key_points: Trump: one bridge or power plant destroyed for every Iranian shot at a ship
A US strike already hit Iran's Larak island in the Strait
Iran vows to hit regional 'infrastructure and bridges' back
Tanker traffic through the Strait falls to a 3-week low

depth.background: The Strait of Hormuz is a narrow sea channel between Iran and the Arabian Peninsula that roughly a fifth of the world's oil and a big share of its gas passes through every day — there's no practical way around it. Iran has effectively been at war with the US and its allies over control of this chokepoint for weeks: Washington wants it kept open for global shipping, Iran has been attacking vessels to pressure the US and Israel to back off. Three American troops were killed and the US has already bombed Iranian targets once to try to reopen the route.

thread.previously: We told you the US and Iran had moved from a stand-off to direct combat: three US troops were killed, and Washington struck Iranian targets to try to force the Strait of Hormuz back open to oil traffic.
thread.changed: Trump has now issued a standing threat: every time Iran fires on a ship in the Strait, the US will destroy one bridge or power plant in Iran, including near Tehran. A US strike already hit the Iranian island of Larak in the Strait, and Iran's government says over 50 people have been killed by US strikes in the past month. Iran has vowed to hit 'infrastructure and bridges across the region' in response, and shipping through the Strait has dropped to a three-week low as tankers avoid the risk.

prev_ref.title: The US and Iran are now trading direct strikes — three American troops killed, and Washington bombs Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: India's electronics production grows 7x in a decade to ₹13.11 lakh crore, creating nearly 25 lakh jobs
stories.what_happened: The Ministry of Electronics and IT and Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said India's electronics production has grown roughly sevenfold in a decade, from about ₹1.9 lakh crore in 2014-15 to ₹13.11 lakh crore in 2025-26, and now supports close to 25 lakh (2.5 million) jobs. Mobile phone production alone rose 33-fold to ₹6.27 lakh crore and mobile exports rose 165-fold to ₹2.59 lakh crore; India now makes about 99.2% of the phones it sells domestically. Electronics has climbed from India's 7th-largest export category to its 3rd-largest.
key_points: India's electronics output grew 7x in a decade to ₹13.11 lakh crore
Nearly 25 lakh jobs created; mobile output up 33x
India now makes 99.2% of the phones it sells at home
Electronics climbed from India's 7th to 3rd-biggest export

depth.background: A decade ago India barely made electronics at home — it mostly imported finished phones and components, and manufacturing here was seen as too capital-intensive to compete with China and Vietnam. Since 2014, the government has layered in tariff protection, tax breaks, labour-law tweaks and the Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme, a program that pays companies a cash bonus for hitting manufacturing and sales targets in India rather than importing.

stories.title: Jaffna Kings hold their nerve to beat Colombo Kaps by 5 runs in a Lanka Premier League thriller
stories.what_happened: In Match 7 of the 2026 Lanka Premier League at Dambulla, Jaffna Kings scored 179/6 in their 20 overs. Colombo Kaps came up 5 runs short in the chase, finishing on 174/9, with Chamindu Wickramasinghe named Player of the Match for his contribution.
key_points: Jaffna posted 179/6; Colombo Kaps fell 5 runs short chasing it down
Chamindu Wickramasinghe was named Player of the Match
Colombo needed 44 off the last 18 balls and couldn't quite get there
Result tightens the middle of the LPL points table

depth.background: The Lanka Premier League is Sri Lanka's domestic T20 franchise tournament, similar in format to the IPL but smaller in scale, and an important stage for Sri Lankan players to build their international case.

stories.title: Southern Brave sweep both men's and women's games against bottom-of-the-table Welsh Fire
stories.what_happened: In a Wednesday doubleheader at Southampton's Utilita Bowl, Southern Brave Women beat Welsh Fire Women by 39 runs, posting 166-3 before restricting Fire to 127-4. In the men's match later the same day, Southern Brave Men bowled Welsh Fire out for just 97 in their 100 balls, then chased it down comfortably, winning by 42 runs after posting 139-5.
key_points: Southern Brave Women won by 39 runs: 166-3 to Welsh Fire's 127-4
Southern Brave Men won by 42 runs: 139-5 to Welsh Fire's 97 all out
Both Welsh Fire sides sit bottom of their respective Hundred tables
Part of a Southampton doubleheader in England's franchise competition, The Hundred

depth.background: The Hundred is England's short-format franchise cricket competition — each innings is 100 balls instead of the 120 balls of a standard T20 — running men's and women's tournaments in parallel with eight city-based franchise teams. Southern Brave and Welsh Fire are two of those franchises; Welsh Fire's men's and women's sides have both struggled this season.

stories.title: Mexico's Temoac mayor is shot dead inside his own town hall — months after surviving a first attempt
stories.what_happened: On Wednesday, July 22, two armed men entered Temoac's Palacio Municipal (town hall) just after noon and opened fire on Lavín inside the building, killing him. The attackers fled on a motorcycle. Morelos state security forces launched a search of Temoac and surrounding communities, but by the next day no arrests had been announced and no motive had been officially confirmed.
key_points: Two gunmen walked into Temoac's municipal building and shot Mayor Valentín Lavín Romero
He had survived an earlier assassination attempt in January
The killers escaped on a motorcycle; no arrests yet
He is the 3rd Mexican mayor murdered in 2026, the 14th since Oct 2024

depth.background: Temoac is a small town in the central state of Morelos, part of a swath of Mexico where local drug-trafficking and extortion rackets often fight for control of town governments. Mayors are frequent targets because they control local police, permits, and municipal contracts — control worth killing for. Valentín Lavín Romero, aligned with President Claudia Sheinbaum's ruling Morena party, survived a shooting attempt on his life in January 2026.

stories.title: Russia bombs Black Sea grain ships; Ukraine calls emergency UN meeting as the corridor goes to zero traffic
stories.what_happened: Russia has bombed at least three civilian cargo ships in the Black Sea in the past few days, including a Kh-59/Kh-69 cruise-missile strike on the cargo ship Golden Leo on July 19 that killed 10 crew as it left Ukraine's grain corridor loaded with grain. Ukraine's acting foreign minister Andrii Sybiha said Wednesday that zero vessels passed through the corridor that day — right at the peak of the summer harvest — and called for an emergency UN Security Council meeting, now set for July 27.
key_points: Russia bombed 3+ civilian cargo ships in the Black Sea this week
10 crew killed when missiles hit grain ship Golden Leo
Zero ships crossed Ukraine's grain corridor Wednesday — peak harvest season
Ukraine calls emergency UN Security Council meeting for July 27

depth.background: Since Russia's 2022 invasion, Ukraine has depended on a negotiated 'safe corridor' through the Black Sea to keep exporting grain, sunflower oil and other food that much of Africa, the Middle East and Asia rely on. Russia has repeatedly threatened or attacked that corridor when talks stall, and this spike in ship strikes lands just as the separate US-brokered Iran ceasefire is also collapsing — straining global shipping and insurance markets on two fronts at once.

stories.title: Aston Martin's season-defining Hungary upgrade arrives late, with barely enough spare parts for both cars
stories.what_happened: The upgraded car for Fernando Alonso and Lance Stroll arrived late — reportedly not ready until the evening before track action began, leaving mechanics far less time than usual to fit and check it. Aston Martin says the delay traces to outdated, inherited project-management and engineering-simulation tools that weren't built for a car designed under 2026's wholesale rules reset, plus vibration issues and battery failures that ate into the team's resources. Team principal Mike Krack said supplying enough spare parts to run the new package on both cars all weekend is 'a huge task.'
key_points: Season-defining upgrade for Alonso and Stroll arrives late
Outdated tools plus vibration and battery issues caused the delay
Team may lack spare parts to run it on both cars
Boss Krack calls the logistics 'a huge task'

depth.background: Aston Martin has scored just one point all season and bet its entire 2026 turnaround on a single, season-defining upgrade — a new chassis, nose and reworked aerodynamics — landing at the Hungarian Grand Prix, the last race before F1's three-week summer break. Yesterday's coverage described that upgrade landing as planned.

thread.previously: Aston Martin was bringing its biggest upgrade of the season — new chassis, new nose, reworked aero — to Hungary, betting its season on the car working.
thread.changed: The upgrade has arrived late: parts weren't ready until the evening before track running, cutting into prep time, and the team may not have enough spare parts to run it on both cars all weekend.

prev_ref.title: Aston Martin's biggest upgrade of the season lands in Hungary — new chassis, new nose, reworked aero

stories.title: Pakistan's under-19 women level their T20 series with South Africa after a nervy 6-wicket win
stories.what_happened: At the National Bank Stadium in Karachi on July 22, South Africa Women U19 were restricted to 109 for 8 in their 20 overs. Pakistan Women U19 chased the target down with six wickets in hand, powered by Zoofishan Ayyaz's unbeaten 44, to level the five-match series 2-2 ahead of the decider.
key_points: Pakistan Women U19 beat South Africa Women U19 by 6 wickets in the 4th T20I in Karachi
South Africa posted 109/8; Zoofishan Ayyaz's unbeaten 44 guided Pakistan's chase
The 5-match series is now level at 2-2 with one match left
Series has swung back and forth — South Africa won games 1 and 3, Pakistan games 2 and 4

depth.background: Pakistan and South Africa's under-19 women's teams are playing a five-match T20I series at the National Bank Stadium in Karachi, running July 14–24, 2026 — the kind of age-group series both boards use to spot and develop future senior internationals.

stories.title: The Pentagon just handed a quantum-computing startup its biggest US government award yet
stories.what_happened: PsiQuantum, a Bay Area quantum-computing startup, signed a $125 million agreement with DARPA on July 22 to fund expanded testing of its hardware, software, and manufacturing — including its Milpitas, California and Chicago, Illinois facilities. The deal builds on a smaller $31.8 million DARPA award from September 2025 and makes PsiQuantum one of only two companies to reach Stage C, the final validation stage, of DARPA's Quantum Benchmarking Initiative. It's the largest US government award the company has received to date.
key_points: PsiQuantum signs a $125 million expanded deal with DARPA, the Pentagon's research arm
Money funds independent verification of PsiQuantum's hardware and manufacturing
It's 1 of just 2 companies to reach the final stage of DARPA's quantum benchmarking program
Builds on a much smaller $31.8 million award from September 2025 — a big vote of confidence

depth.background: Classical computers store information as 0s or 1s. Quantum computers use 'qubits,' which can hold both at once, letting them (in theory) crack certain problems classical computers never could — but nobody has yet built one 'useful' enough to matter outside a lab. DARPA (the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency) runs a program called the Quantum Benchmarking Initiative specifically to independently test whether any company's approach can realistically deliver a genuinely useful quantum computer by 2033, rather than take companies' own claims at face value. PsiQuantum builds its qubits from photons — particles of light — instead of the superconducting circuits or trapped ions rival companies like IBM, Google, and IonQ use.

stories.title: Zomato's parent Eternal posts a 268% profit jump as its Blinkit quick-commerce arm outgrows food delivery
stories.what_happened: Eternal reported Q1 FY27 (April–June 2026) results on July 22: revenue from operations grew to ₹20,211 crore from ₹7,167 crore a year earlier — up 182% — while net profit jumped nearly 3.7x to ₹92 crore. Blinkit alone brought in ₹15,664 crore, or 77.5% of total revenue, overtaking food delivery as the company's main business. Zomato's food-delivery arm grew 37% year-on-year to ₹3,100 crore. Revenue also grew 17% quarter-on-quarter from ₹17,634 crore in Q4 FY26.
key_points: Eternal Q1 FY27 net profit up 268% year-on-year to ₹92 crore
Revenue more than doubled — up 182% — to ₹20,211 crore
Blinkit (quick commerce) is now 77.5% of revenue, bigger than food delivery
Zomato's own food-delivery business grew a slower but solid 37%

depth.background: Eternal is the company formerly known as Zomato, renamed in 2025 as it expanded beyond food delivery into quick commerce (10–20 minute grocery and essentials delivery) through its Blinkit arm. Quick commerce has become the fastest-growing and most capital-intensive part of India's consumer internet sector, with Blinkit, Zepto and Swiggy Instamart racing to build dark stores (small local warehouses) across cities.

stories.title: Namibia Women win a Super Over thriller against Uganda in a T20 Quadrangular Series tie
stories.what_happened: Namibia Women and Uganda Women finished tied on 123 for 7 apiece after their full 20 overs, sending the match to a Super Over — cricket's version of a shootout, where each team faces one extra over to settle the result. Namibia won the Super Over to take the match. In the tournament's other fixture that day, Hong Kong Women beat Tanzania Women by 16 runs after Tanzania set a target of 136.
key_points: Namibia and Uganda both scored 123/7 — match finished tied after 20 overs each
Namibia won the Super Over (cricket's tie-breaker) to take the match
In the day's other fixture, Hong Kong Women beat Tanzania Women by 16 runs
Part of a T20 Quadrangular Series being hosted in Namibia

depth.background: A Quadrangular Series is a tournament between four national teams, each playing the others in a round-robin. This one, hosted in Namibia, features the Namibia, Uganda, Hong Kong and Tanzania women's teams — nations that don't get frequent international fixtures, making tournaments like this important for their players' match experience and world rankings.

stories.title: Ferrari boss says the Hungaroring could finally suit his car — but he's not celebrating yet
stories.what_happened: Ahead of this weekend's Hungarian Grand Prix — the final race before F1's mid-season break — Ferrari team principal Fred Vasseur said the Hungaroring's characteristics should better suit the SF-26 than recent circuits. He pointed to Ferrari's recent uptick in form (Hamilton's win in Barcelona, Leclerc's win at Silverstone, and a Leclerc podium finish at Spa) as evidence of real progress, while cautioning that a favorable track alone doesn't guarantee a result.
key_points: Fred Vasseur says Hungaroring's tight, twisty layout should favor Ferrari's SF-26
Ferrari has quietly picked up form: a Hamilton win in Barcelona, a Leclerc win at Silverstone, a Leclerc podium at Spa
Vasseur still warns 'that alone is no guarantee' of a good result
This is the last race before F1's summer break

depth.background: Formula 1 cars are tuned differently for different tracks — some favor long straights and top speed, others favor tight corners and downforce. The Hungaroring, this weekend's venue, is a slow, twisty circuit often compared to a 'go-kart track' because overtaking is hard and car handling through corners matters more than raw power. Ferrari's SF-26 has generally been considered a stronger car at technical, corner-heavy circuits than at high-speed ones this season.

stories.title: West Indies coach Daren Sammy backs injured captain Roston Chase to be fit for the first Pakistan Test
stories.what_happened: Head coach Daren Sammy said he's confident Chase will recover in time for the series-opening Test against Pakistan, which starts July 25 in Trinidad. Chase missed the New Zealand ODIs with swelling in the injured finger, but Sammy said the team's medical staff has a plan to keep him on the field, calling Chase 'a fighter' who won't want to miss Test cricket.
key_points: Chase has a finger injury that ruled him out of the recent New Zealand ODI series
Head coach Daren Sammy says he's confident Chase will recover in time
First Test against Pakistan starts July 25 in Trinidad, with valuable World Test Championship points on the line
Chase is both West Indies' captain and their main spin-bowling all-rounder, making his fitness a big swing factor

depth.background: Roston Chase captains West Indies in Test cricket and is also their frontline spin-bowling all-rounder, meaning his absence hurts the team twice over — in leadership and in the XI's balance. He sustained an injury to his right index finger that kept him out of West Indies' recently finished three-match ODI series against New Zealand. West Indies now host Pakistan for a two-match Test series that counts toward the World Test Championship (WTC) — a league table run over a two-year cycle where results feed into rankings and eventually a final, and where both these sides currently sit near the bottom.

stories.title: TMC's Kalyan Banerjee suspended from the Lok Sabha after a clash with MPs who defected to a new party
stories.what_happened: On Wednesday, Banerjee got into a heated verbal exchange with NCPI MPs — his former TMC colleagues — shortly after the House adjourned, reportedly involving objectionable remarks directed at a woman NCPI MP. NCPI MPs complained in writing to Speaker Om Birla, who directed the government to act; a resolution to suspend Banerjee for the rest of the session was moved and passed by voice vote, and he was ordered to leave the Parliament premises immediately.
key_points: Trinamool Congress MP Kalyan Banerjee was suspended from the Lok Sabha for the rest of the Monsoon Session
He clashed with MPs from NCPI — a new party formed by 20 former TMC MPs who defected after Mamata Banerjee's crushing state-election defeat
The Speaker acted on a written complaint from NCPI MPs over remarks made during the altercation
It's the first floor clash between TMC's loyalist rump and the breakaway bloc since the split

depth.background: In May 2026, the BJP won a landslide 206 of 293 seats in the West Bengal state election, ending Mamata Banerjee's 15-year rule as chief minister. The shock triggered a rebellion inside her own party's Lok Sabha benches in Delhi: 20 of TMC's 28 MPs broke away and merged into NCPI (Nationalist Citizens Party of India), a smaller party, in a move designed to clear the two-thirds-of-members threshold that lets lawmakers switch parties without being disqualified under India's anti-defection law. That merger made NCPI the Lok Sabha's fifth-largest party and a new ally of the BJP-led government, while just 8 MPs — including Kalyan Banerjee — stayed loyal to Mamata and Abhishek Banerjee.

stories.title: India's Kuldeep Yadav signs with Yorkshire after being left out of the XI for India's England ODI series
stories.what_happened: Yorkshire County Cricket Club announced on Wednesday, July 22 that they have signed Kuldeep Yadav as their overseas player for the rest of the season. He will play in five Metro Bank One-Day Cup matches, starting against Glamorgan, before returning to Yorkshire's home ground, Headingley, to play three of the team's final four County Championship (first-class) matches in September. Yorkshire's general manager of cricket, Gavin Hamilton, said Kuldeep's left-arm wrist spin and international experience add a valuable dimension to the squad.
key_points: Yorkshire sign Kuldeep as overseas player for the One-Day Cup and County Championship
Comes after Kuldeep didn't play a single game in India's 2-1 ODI series loss in England
He'll play five One-Day Cup matches before three County Championship games in September
Yorkshire's GM calls him an 'accomplished, international-quality' spinner

depth.background: Kuldeep Yadav is one of India's premier wrist-spin bowlers, but he was an unused squad member during India's recent ODI tour of England, which India lost 2-1. County cricket (England's domestic competition) is a common stop for international players between international assignments, letting them stay match-fit and earn extra pay while giving county sides star quality.

stories.title: US diesel prices hit $5.13 a gallon as the Iran war works its way into everyday costs
stories.what_happened: The US Energy Information Administration's weekly benchmark for diesel jumped nearly 34 cents to $5.13 a gallon — the sharpest one-week rise since the war's first week in March — while the average US gasoline price climbed 15 cents to $3.94 and looks set to cross $4 again. CNBC's July 22 market analysis links the move directly to the ongoing Iran conflict and the Gulf shipping risk it creates.
key_points: Diesel jumped ~34 cents in a week to $5.13/gallon — biggest weekly jump since March
Gasoline is back near $4/gallon, up 15 cents in a week
Diesel prices set the 'fuel surcharges' truckers and airlines charge
Higher freight costs eventually show up in grocery and goods prices

depth.background: Diesel and jet fuel react more sharply than gasoline to Middle East supply scares because a larger share of Gulf refining capacity is skewed toward the heavier fuels used in trucking, shipping, and aviation. The US-Iran war, which began in March and has driven repeated spikes in Gulf shipping risk, has already pushed diesel from roughly $3.56 a gallon before the war to today's levels.

stories.title: The US government hands nearly 300 AI science projects to nine national labs — and Meta's free vision models are already doing the heavy lifting
stories.what_happened: The DOE announced awards for nearly 300 Genesis Mission projects spread across national labs including Berkeley, Argonne, Brookhaven, and Oak Ridge. Berkeley Lab is leading a flagship effort called SYNAPS-I, which pairs Meta's DINOv3 (which recognizes what's in an image and roughly where) with SAM (which draws exact pixel-by-pixel outlines around each object) to automatically analyze scientific images. Running across 300 AI chips (Nvidia A100 GPUs) at four labs, the first application analyzes 3D micro-CT scans of grapevines to understand how they respond to drought at a cellular level — turning roughly a month of manual labeling work into about 15 minutes.
key_points: The Department of Energy names almost 300 'Genesis Mission' projects across nine national labs
Berkeley Lab's flagship project, SYNAPS-I, uses Meta's free SAM and DINOv3 AI models to read lab images
The pipeline compresses a month of manual scientific image-labeling into about 15 minutes
First target: how grapevines survive drought, using micro-CT scans of plant tissue

depth.background: The US Department of Energy runs 17 national laboratories that do frontier science — nuclear physics, materials, biology, climate. A huge amount of that work involves humans manually poring over images from microscopes, X-ray scanners, and telescopes to label what they're looking at, which is slow and expensive. In 2025 the White House announced the 'Genesis Mission,' a push to use artificial intelligence to speed up that bottleneck across the whole national lab system. Meta, better known for Instagram and WhatsApp, has for several years released its AI models for free ('open-weight') rather than locking them up, including a family of vision models called Segment Anything (SAM) and DINOv3.

stories.title: A 1,000-acre wildfire near San Francisco is being brought under control
stories.what_happened: The Little Fire ignited around 2:30 p.m. local time on Tuesday, July 21, near Pleasanton in Alameda County, and grew quickly to over 1,000 acres. It prompted evacuation orders in nearby neighbourhoods. By Wednesday morning, firefighters had stopped its forward spread and reached 40% containment, allowing some evacuation orders to be downgraded from mandatory to advisory.
key_points: The 'Little Fire' has burned over 1,007 acres near Pleasanton and Sunol, California
It started Tuesday afternoon; firefighters had it 40% contained by Wednesday morning
Some mandatory evacuation orders near the Ruby Hill neighbourhood were downgraded to advisory overnight
One firefighter was injured; forward progress of the fire has been stopped

depth.background: California's dry summer hills, especially in the East Bay area around Pleasanton and Sunol, are classic wildfire terrain — grass and brush that cure to tinder by midsummer, combined with wind, can turn a small spark into a fast-moving blaze within hours. Fires like this have become a near-annual feature of California summers as the fire season has lengthened with warmer, drier conditions.

stories.title: Dambulla Sixers chase down 179 to beat Galle Gallants by 6 wickets in the Lanka Premier League
stories.what_happened: Dambulla Sixers chased down Galle Gallants' total of 178 for 8 with 23 balls to spare, finishing on 180 for 4 in 16.1 overs to win by 6 wickets in the tournament's 8th match, played July 22.
key_points: Galle Gallants posted 178/8; Dambulla ran it down in 16.1 overs
Dambulla's 6-wicket win keeps them firmly in the LPL playoff hunt
Their second win in as many days after beating Kandy Royals
One of several LPL group-stage games this week shaping the semi-final race

depth.background: The Lanka Premier League (LPL) is Sri Lanka's franchise Twenty20 (T20) tournament — the Sri Lankan equivalent of India's IPL — running through late July with teams from Sri Lankan cities competing for a place in the semi-finals and final.

stories.title: Ferrari's Leclerc stuns McLaren with a shock pole at the Hungarian Grand Prix
stories.what_happened: In qualifying for the Hungarian Grand Prix, Charles Leclerc set a lap of 1 minute 15.372 seconds in the final Q3 session, beating championship leader Oscar Piastri by just 0.026 seconds and Lando Norris by a further 0.015 seconds, to claim a surprise pole position. Norris had actually set the fastest time of the whole session in Q2, but changing weather — clouds rolling in, wind picking up, and dropping track temperature — meant he couldn't repeat it when it mattered in Q3. Lewis Hamilton failed to make it out of Q2.
key_points: Charles Leclerc took pole with a 1:15.372 lap, beating Piastri by 0.026 seconds
It's Ferrari's first pole of the season for a full grand prix
McLaren's Norris was fastest in Q2 but couldn't repeat it as conditions changed in Q3
Hamilton was knocked out in Q2, well down the grid

depth.background: McLaren has dominated most of this F1 season, with Oscar Piastri and Lando Norris regularly locking out the front of the grid. Ferrari brought upgraded suspension to the car at last week's Belgian Grand Prix, and Hungary — a tight, twisty circuit that rewards a well-balanced car over raw engine power — has historically suited Ferrari, though the team hadn't converted that into a pole this year until now.

stories.title: The US signs a nuclear-power deal with Saudi Arabia that could let it enrich its own fuel — and Israel is alarmed
stories.what_happened: On Wednesday, US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and Saudi Energy Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman signed a peaceful nuclear cooperation agreement — known in US law as a '123 Agreement' — along with a related safeguards pact. The deal, which runs 30 years, creates a legal path for Saudi Arabia to eventually enrich its own nuclear fuel rather than simply buying finished fuel from abroad. Crucially, it was signed without the Saudi-Israel normalization deal that US officials had previously floated as part of the same package.
key_points: US Energy Secretary and Saudi Energy Minister signed a '123 Agreement' on civil nuclear cooperation
The deal opens a path for Saudi Arabia to enrich uranium on its own soil
Israel says a Saudi enrichment program could become a stepping stone to nuclear weapons
The deal comes without the Israel-Saudi normalization pact that was originally meant to be attached

depth.background: For years, Washington and Riyadh have discussed civilian nuclear cooperation, but talks were long tied to a bigger prize: getting Saudi Arabia to formally normalize relations with Israel, similar to the 2020 Abraham Accords. Saudi Arabia has said it wants nuclear power to diversify away from oil and meet growing electricity demand, but critics have always worried that enrichment technology can be used for both power plants and weapons.

stories.title: Sri Lanka's under-19s fight to save the Youth Test in Colombo after India's first-innings 411
stories.what_happened: By the close of play on day 3 of the four-day Youth Test in Colombo (July 20–23), Sri Lanka's Under-19s were batting again — this time in their second innings, having been made to follow on after India's first-innings 411. Vimath Dinsara held firm on 55 not out as Sri Lanka reached 155 for 4, trying to bat out the match and avoid an innings defeat.
key_points: Sri Lanka U19, following on after India's 411, reached 155/4 in their second innings by day 3
Vimath Dinsara battled to an unbeaten 55 to anchor the rebuild
It's a bounce-back from Sri Lanka being reduced to 122/8 the day before
The 4-day Youth Test in Colombo runs July 20–23

depth.background: This is the second Youth Test between the India and Sri Lanka Under-19 teams, part of a tour that also included a multi-day series and is being used to develop the next generation of players for both boards.

thread.previously: India's Under-19s had piled up 411 in their first innings, with centuries from Kushagra Ojha and Manal Chauhan, and had reduced Sri Lanka's Under-19s to a struggling 122/8 in reply.
thread.changed: Sri Lanka's first innings folded well behind India's total, forcing them to follow on. By the close of day 3, in their second innings, Sri Lanka had rebuilt to 155 for 4, with Vimath Dinsara unbeaten on 55, fighting to save the match from an innings defeat.

prev_ref.title: India's Under-19s pile up 411 and reduce Sri Lanka to 122/8 — young batters Kushagra Ojha and Manal Chauhan both score centuries

stories.title: Afghanistan names Rahmat Shah as new ODI and Test captain after Hashmatullah Shahidi steps down
stories.what_happened: The Afghanistan Cricket Board named Rahmat Shah as the new ODI and Test captain, replacing Hashmatullah Shahidi, who announced his decision to step down on social media. Wicketkeeper-batter Rahmanullah Gurbaz was named vice-captain across both formats. Rahmat, who debuted internationally in 2013 and has played 128 ODIs and 12 Tests, takes over immediately, with his first assignment being an ODI series against Ireland starting August 5.
key_points: Rahmat Shah named Afghanistan's new ODI and Test captain
Replaces Hashmatullah Shahidi, who stepped down
Rahmanullah Gurbaz named vice-captain in both formats
First assignment: ODI series vs Ireland from August 5

depth.background: Afghanistan's cricket team has become one of the most competitive sides in the world even amid turbulent leadership changes, highlighted by a memorable run to the Super Eight/knockout stage of the 2023 ODI World Cup. Hashmatullah Shahidi led the side through that stretch, with Rahmat Shah serving as his vice-captain.

stories.title: Parliament's third day of chaos: Lok Sabha adjourned again as government says it's ready to discuss NEET leak, blames Congress for conditions
stories.what_happened: For a third straight day, both houses of Parliament saw repeated disruptions and adjournments as opposition MPs pressed for an immediate debate on the NEET leak and the crackdown on protesting students. Kiren Rijiju said the government was ready to hold the discussion, but blamed Congress for attaching conditions that were blocking it from starting. The Lok Sabha was adjourned to reconvene later in the day.
key_points: Lok Sabha proceedings were adjourned again on day 3 of the Monsoon Session over the NEET paper-leak row
Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju says the government is willing to discuss the NEET leak in the House
Rijiju accuses Congress of 'needlessly putting conditions' before agreeing to the debate
Deputy Chairman Harivansh urged MPs to reach consensus and take up the education discussion 'in a civil manner'

depth.background: The Monsoon Session, which began July 21, has been paralyzed from day one by opposition demands for a debate on the NEET (India's national medical entrance exam) paper-leak scandal, and by anger over last week's police crackdown on the 'Cockroach' student protest movement in Delhi. Congress leaders Rahul and Priyanka Gandhi were themselves briefly detained by Delhi Police during a march to PM Modi's residence over the issue, and education activist Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike over the same demands has stretched past three weeks.

thread.previously: On day 2, both houses were repeatedly disrupted and eventually adjourned for the day as opposition MPs demanded debate on the NEET paper leak and Monday's crackdown on the 'Cockroach' protest march; Congress also alleged Parliament gates were sealed and internet suspended during the protest.
thread.changed: On day 3, the government's Kiren Rijiju said it is now willing to hold the NEET debate, but accused Congress of blocking it with conditions — shifting the fight from whether to debate the issue to how.

prev_ref.title: Parliament's Monsoon Session descends into chaos — repeatedly adjourned as Opposition demands answers on NEET leak, Ram Mandir donations and the Cockroach protest crackdown

stories.title: Opposition wears black in Parliament, demands Amit Shah and Dharmendra Pradhan resign over the crackdown on protesting students
stories.what_happened: On Wednesday, opposition MPs wore black clothing into both Houses of Parliament to protest Monday's police action against student protesters. Congress leader Rahul Gandhi laid out five demands: an apology from Prime Minister Modi, the resignations of Home Minister Amit Shah and Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, accountability for the officers involved, withdrawal of cases filed against the students, and a formal parliamentary debate on the incident. Both the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha were adjourned again as the standoff continued.
key_points: Congress-led opposition wore black on Day 3 of the Monsoon Session
Trigger: police used batons on students marching to Parliament over exam paper leaks
Rahul Gandhi wants PM Modi to apologise and Shah and Pradhan to quit
Both Houses adjourned again amid the standoff

depth.background: India's Monsoon Session of Parliament opened this week already under pressure from student protests over exam paper leaks and the long-running NEET (medical entrance exam) controversy. On Monday, students marching toward Parliament in a 'Chalo Sansad' (roughly, 'let's go to Parliament') demonstration were met with a police baton charge, which the opposition says was excessive.

stories.title: Lok Sabha takes up a bill to expand the Supreme Court from 33 to 37 judges, replacing May's ordinance
stories.what_happened: Union Law Minister Arjun Ram Meghwal introduced the Supreme Court (Number of Judges) Amendment Bill, 2026 in the Lok Sabha, formally converting May's ordinance into permanent law and raising the Supreme Court's sanctioned judge strength from 33 to 37 (a figure that excludes the Chief Justice of India). The Lok Sabha is taking it up for consideration and passage this week during the ongoing Monsoon Session.
key_points: Bill raises the Supreme Court's sanctioned strength from 33 to 37 judges (excluding the CJI)
It formally replaces an ordinance the government issued back in May
Law Minister Arjun Ram Meghwal is steering it through the Lok Sabha this week
Aimed at cutting the court's long-standing case backlog

depth.background: The number of judges allowed on India's Supreme Court isn't fixed by the Constitution — Parliament sets it by law, and has raised it several times before as the court's caseload grew. In May 2026, the government used an ordinance (a temporary law the President can issue when Parliament isn't in session) to raise the court's strength from 33 to 37 judges. Under India's Constitution, an ordinance automatically lapses a set number of weeks after Parliament reconvenes unless lawmakers pass it as a regular Act — which is exactly what this bill now does.

stories.title: Supreme Court refuses to block 6 Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs' merger into Shinde's party — but agrees to hear the challenge
stories.what_happened: On 18 July, Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla approved the merger of six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs — Sanjay Deshmukh, Sanjay Bandu Jadhav, Sanjay Dina Patil, Nagesh Patil-Ashtikar, Omprakash Rajenimbalkar and Bhausaheb Wakchaure — into Eknath Shinde's Shiv Sena. UBT, through leader Arvind Sawant, challenged that decision in the Supreme Court, arguing the six were elected on the UBT ticket and that the Speaker had no basis to recognize a 'merger' of just six individuals into a rival party. A bench of Justices P.S. Narasimha and Alok Aradhe declined to pause the Speaker's decision but issued formal notice to the Speaker's office and the six MPs, directing them to respond, with the case returning in about two weeks.
key_points: Shiv Sena (UBT) asked the Supreme Court to block the Speaker's recognition of 6 of its Lok Sabha MPs merging into Eknath Shinde's rival Shiv Sena
The Court declined to stay the Speaker's decision but issued notice, with the next hearing in two weeks
The merger cut Shiv Sena (UBT)'s Lok Sabha strength to just 3 MPs
It's the same anti-defection-law fight now playing out for TMC too, this time reaching the Lok Sabha instead of a state assembly

depth.background: The Shiv Sena split in 2022, when Eknath Shinde broke away from Uddhav Thackeray's Shiv Sena with a large group of legislators; the Election Commission later recognized Shinde's faction as the 'real' Shiv Sena, keeping the party's name and bow-and-arrow symbol, while Thackeray's remnant became known as Shiv Sena (UBT). The two sides have been locked in overlapping legal and political battles ever since, mostly over state assembly seats — this new fight is about Lok Sabha MPs instead.

stories.title: Alex Albon becomes the first driver to make 100 race starts for Williams
